blurb: Lockdown Mode is an extreme optional protection designed for individuals at heightened risk of sophisticated state-sponsored cyberattacks. When enabled, it dramatically reduces the attack surface by disabling most message attachment types and link previews, blocking FaceTime calls from unknown contacts, preventing wired connections to accessories when the device is locked, and disabling installation of configuration profiles. Web content is also restricted from executing complex JavaScript via the JIT compiler. Lockdown Mode trades functionality for the highest available attack-surface reduction on iPhone, iPad, and Mac.
